issues.body: `_protected_default_tip` validates only `commit.sha`; it never checks the branches API response's `protected` field. Thus a repository whose default branch is unprotected (or becomes unprotected) is treated as a protected trust source, allowing routing changes outside the claimed branch-protected control surface. This is a fail-open trust check. The test fixture supplies `"protected": True`, but no assertion depends on it: changing that value to false or deleting it still passes, so the protection leg is vacuous.
issues.fix_hint: Require the branch response to report `protected is True` before accepting its SHA and raise `DispatchError` otherwise. Add negative self-tests for both `protected: false` and a missing/malformed protection field, asserting each is rejected; those assertions should fail if the production protection check is removed.
issues.title: Default-branch routing is accepted without proving branch protection

verdict.summary: The new lookup removes the plan-controlled SHA from routing selection, but it does not establish the property its API and comments claim: that the selected default-branch tip is protected. Because the returned branch protection state is ignored, CLAIM can trust routing from an unprotected branch. The self-test is correspondingly unable to detect this weakened trust boundary.
